title: Do not default to the centered authentication card with decorative background
summary: AI-generated authentication screens consistently use a centered card on a decorative gradient or geometric background, with logo, email+password fields, social login buttons, and a 'Forgot password?' link.
instruction: Choose an authentication layout that matches the app's context and security requirements. Consider inline authentication, passwordless entry, or context-specific login instead of the universal centered card on a decorative background.
rationale: The centered auth card on gradient background is a default pattern that wastes vertical space, adds social login choice overload at the point of entry, and decorates a security-sensitive interaction with meaningless visuals.
violations: Centered card containing logo, email input, password input with eye toggle, and 'Sign In' button
Decorative gradient, mesh, or geometric pattern background unrelated to brand
'Or continue with' divider with Google and GitHub icon buttons
'Forgot password?' link positioned below the password field
Card does not fill viewport height, leaving large empty margins on desktop
controls: Inline authentication within the app context (no full-page takeover)
Passwordless authentication with magic link as the primary method
Minimal authentication with a single input field and no decorative background
borderlineCases: Consumer apps where social login is the primary entry method
contextualExceptions: Enterprise apps where the centered card is an expected convention
detectorSignal: A centered card containing credential input fields with a decorative background (gradient or pattern) and social login provider icons below a divider.
matcherProposal: Detect a centered form card on a full-viewport gradient/pattern background with email and password inputs, plus social login buttons.
detectorLimitations: Cannot distinguish between intentional social-login-first design and template defaulting
falsePositiveScenarios: Consumer apps where social login is genuinely the primary path
mitigationStrategy: Flag with context about whether the background and social login row add value or are decorative defaults.

fixturePlans.violation: Centered auth card with purple gradient background, email+password fields, eye toggle, social login row, and 'Forgot password?' link.
fixturePlans.control: Inline login form within an existing page, no decorative background, single method promoted.
fixturePlans.borderline: Consumer app where social login is the primary expected method.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Partially related to color.purple-gradient-default when the background uses purple-indigo gradients.
